optimizations: Review part geometry for opportunities to simplify features and reduce machining complexity ($50-$100 savings).
Consider using a more efficient 4-axis machining strategy, such as using a ball nose endmill instead of a square endmill ($75-$150 savings).
risks: High machining costs may be due to complex geometry or inefficient toolpath.
